ace: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Acehnese.
acne: noun. A skin condition, usually of the face, that is common in adolescents.
aeon: noun. Commonwealth standard spelling of eon.
ancon: noun. The corner of a wall or rafter.
ane: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Xârâcùù.
announce: verb. To give public notice of, especially for the first time; to make known.
cannon: noun. A complete assembly, consisting of an artillery tube and a breech mechanism, firing mechanism or base cap, which is a component of a
canoe: noun. A small long and narrow boat, propelled by one or more people (depending on the size of canoe), using single-bladed paddles.
canon: noun. A generally accepted principle; a rule.
con: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Cofán.
cone: noun. A surface of revolution formed by rotating a segment of a line around another line that intersects the first line.
conn: noun. The duty of directing a ship, usually used with the verb to have or to take and accompanied by the article "the."
cue: noun. The name of the Latin script letter Q / q.
ecu: noun. The European Currency Unit (symbol ₠), a currency used in the European Community before the euro.
eon: noun. Eternity, the duration of the universe.
nae: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Naka'ela.
nan: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Min Nan.
nance: noun. Alternative form of nancy (“an effeminate male homosexual”).
neon: noun. The chemical element (symbol Ne) with an atomic number of 10.
nonce: noun. The one or single occasion; the present reason or purpose.
none: pronoun. Not any of a given number or group.
noun: noun. A word that functions as the name of a specific object or set of objects, such as person, animal, place, word, thing, phenomenon,
nun: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Anong.
oca: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ocaina.
ocean: noun. One of the large bodies of water separating the continents.
once: adverb. One and only one time.
one: noun. NATO & ICAO radiotelephony clear code (spelling-alphabet name) for the digit 1.
